summary: Scanned 24 qualifying HN stories and surfaced 8 fresh product mentions after cooldown filtering.

items.name: GLM-5.2
items.summary: Open-weight Z.AI model with 1M context and long-horizon coding.
items.why_included: Fresh frontier-model release with direct comment interest in its coding and agentic strengths.
items.evidence: Commenters highlighted the open-weight release, 1M-token context claim, and long-task coding focus.

items.name: Paca
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Paca – Lightweight Jira alternative for human-AI collaboration
items.summary: Lightweight Jira alternative for human-AI sprint planning and task assignment.
items.why_included: Directly praised as a better fit than Jira for agentic team workflows.
items.evidence: Comments compared it with Jira, GitHub Issues, Linear, and worktrees, and liked the customization and WASM plugin plan.

items.name: Sunwet
items.source_story_title: The state of building user interfaces in Rust
items.summary: HTML-first Rust UI project built with wasm-bindgen and web-sys.
items.why_included: A commenter called it fun and used it as a concrete alternative in the Rust UI debate.
items.evidence: One commenter said they build UIs in HTML via wasm-bindgen/web-sys, and another said Sunwet looked fun enough to try.

items.name: aur-malware-check
items.source_story_title: Arch Linux Now Believes Malware Incident Under Control: More Than 1,500 Packages
items.summary: Script that flags installed AUR packages against the compromised package list.
items.why_included: Useful incident-response helper surfaced directly in the Arch malware discussion.
items.evidence: Commenters shared the repo plus pacman/yay commands for checking AUR installs against the published affected list.

items.name: pxv
items.summary: xv-inspired image editor for viewing, editing, and clearing EXIF.
items.why_included: A concrete utility for the metadata workflow the thread was discussing.
items.evidence: Commenters named pxv as an EXIF viewer/editor/clearer and contrasted it with the messiness of image metadata tools.

items.name: OwnTracks
items.summary: Private location diary app for tracking where your phone has been.
items.why_included: Strong privacy-oriented tool mention from a practical photo-context discussion.
items.evidence: The thread listed OwnTracks among location-logging tools people use to recover where photos were taken and where they went.

items.name: darktable
items.summary: Open-source RAW photo workflow app that can geotag from GPX.
items.why_included: A mature photo tool that commenters used for post-hoc geotagging.
items.evidence: Commenters described geotagging photos later with a GPX file and named darktable as the software they use.

items.name: Roman Empire map
items.source_story_title: Show HN: I am building a map of people who lived in the Roman Empire
items.summary: Site mapping named Roman-era people from inscription data.
items.why_included: A concrete show-HN project with an unusual AI-assisted research pipeline.
items.evidence: The maker explained the 500k-inscription extraction pipeline and commenters engaged with the linked source databases and quality metrics.
